title: SSC CGL T-I English Section Test

questions.question: Four words are given, out of which only one word is spelt correctly. Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelt word is (d) propriety. • Correct word: propriety means the quality of behaving correctly and according to accepted social or moral standards. (Hindi meaning: शिष्टाचार / मर्यादा) • Example: She always maintained propriety in formal meetings. Why the other options are incorrect: • (a) properiety: incorrect spelling (extra letters). • (b) propatiety: incorrect spelling (wrong vowel and letter order). • (c) propriaty: incorrect spelling (missing letters). So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given phrase. A fish rots from the head down
options: What you started first, you must finish first
The main reason for a failed relationship is lack of trust
The leadership is the root cause when an organisation or state fails
You must end what you started
questions.explanation: • Given phrase – “A fish rots from the head down”: • This proverb means that when something goes wrong in an organisation, system, or country, the real cause usually lies at the top level—i.e., in the leadership/management. • If leaders are corrupt, incompetent, or unethical, the negative impact spreads downward to the rest of the organisation. • (Hindi meaning: मछली सिर से सड़ती है; संगठन की बिगड़ती हालत का कारण नेतृत्व होता है) • Example: If a department becomes corrupt, it often proves that a fish rots from the head down. • Correct meaning – Option (c): • “The leadership is the root cause when an organisation or state fails” exactly captures the proverb’s meaning because it points to the responsibility of the leaders. • Other related idioms/proverbs: • The buck stops here – the final responsibility lies with the leader (Hindi: अंतिम जिम्मेदारी नेतृत्व की होती है) • As the leader, so the people – people follow the example of leaders (Hindi: जैसा राजा, वैसी प्रजा) • Bad apples spoil the barrel – a few bad elements can corrupt the whole group (Hindi: एक खराब व्यक्ति पूरे समूह को बिगाड़ सकता है) • Meanings of other options (why they are incorrect): • (a) What you started first, you must finish first – unrelated to leadership failure. • (b) Failed relationship due to lack of trust – not the meaning of this proverb. • (d) You must end what you started – meaning resembles “finish what you started,” not this phrase. So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. Yesterday, the farmers in Hathras rounded up about 25 abandoning cows and bulls and took them to a dairy in a truck.
questions.explanation: Explanation: "Abandoning cows" is incorrect because “abandoning” means the cows were leaving something, which isn’t the case. The word “abandoning” is incorrect. It is the cows that are being abandoned, not doing the abandoning. The correct adwective is “abandoned cows.” Grammatical rule used: Use the correct past participle adwective when the noun is receiving the action. Example: The abandoned house was spooky. Example: The authorities rescued abandoned puppies from the street.

questions.question: Select the word that is similar in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below Disenable
questions.explanation: The correct synonym of the given word is (d) cripple. • Given word – Disenable: Disenable means to disable or to make someone/something unable to function effectively; to deprive of power or ability. • (Hindi meaning: अक्षम करना / शक्ति या क्षमता छीन लेना) • Example: The inwury disenabled him from performing heavy work for months. • Correct answer word – Cripple: Cripple means to disable severely or to make someone/something weak and ineffective. • (Hindi meaning: अपंग/अक्षम कर देना) • Example: The sudden shortage of funds crippled the prowect. • Synonyms of Disenable: disable, incapacitate, weaken, impair. • Antonyms of Disenable: enable, empower, strengthen, aid. Meanings of all the other given options: • Assist – to help (सहायता करना) • Assuage – to lessen pain or anxiety (कम करना / शांति देना) • Soothe – to calm or comfort (सांत्वना देना / शांत करना) So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Choose correct tense. (1)
questions.explanation: The word that can be filled in the blank is ‘entrenches’ to make the sentence grammatically and contextually correct. • ‘Entrenches’ = firmly establishes something so it becomes difficult to change (हिंदी: “गहराई से स्थापित करना/जड़ जमा देना”). • As per the context, the passage states a general, repeated effect of the habit (“a habit that ___ the mind…”). With a singular subwect “habit,” the simple present verb takes -s → “entrenches” is correct. Meanings of other given words and why they are incorrect: • entrenched – past tense; would restrict the effect to the past, not a general ongoing truth. • has entrenched – present perfect; suggests a completed past action with present relevance, but the sentence is definition-like/general. • entrenching – participle; would require an auxiliary (“is entrenching”) or a different structure.

questions.question: Choose correct verb form. (2)
questions.explanation: The word that can be filled in the blank is ‘perceives’ to make the sentence grammatically and contextually correct. • ‘Perceives’ = sees/understands/regards something in a particular way (हिंदी: “समझना/मानना/अनुभव करना”). • As per the context, “the brain… perceives prolonged reading… as an ordeal” expresses a typical/conditioned response; subwect “brain” is singular, so present simple → “perceives” fits. Meanings of other given words and why they are incorrect: • perceived – simple past; breaks the general present narrative. • perceiving – participle; would need an auxiliary (“is perceiving”). • perceive – base form; would suit plural subwect or require auxiliary (“does perceive”).

questions.question: Choose the correct verb. (3)
questions.explanation: The word that can be filled in the blank is ‘unravel’ to make the sentence grammatically and contextually correct. • ‘Unravel’ = to loosen/undo; to make something fall apart (हिंदी: “उधेड़ देना/बिखेर देना”). • The subwect here is plural: “stray thoughts and pings” (two things woined by ‘and’). Plural subwects take the base verb form in present simple → “unravel” is correct. Meanings of other given words and why they are incorrect: • unravels – singular present; mismatches the plural subwect. • unraveled – past tense; disrupts the present-time general description. • unraveling – participle; needs an auxiliary (“are unraveling”) to be grammatical.

questions.question: Choose the correct descriptive phrase. (4)
questions.explanation: The phrase that can be filled in the blank is ‘quietly incremental’ to make the sentence contextually precise. • ‘Quietly incremental’ = happening slowly, step-by-step, without dramatic notice (हिंदी: “धीरे-धीरे, बिना शोर के क्रमिक”). • As per the context, “erosion of concentration” typically occurs gradually; “quietly incremental erosion” matches the idea of subtle, progressive decline. Meanings of other given options and why they are incorrect: • loudly spectacular – suggests dramatic, noticeable change; opposite of “erosion” happening subtly. • abruptly ceremonial – “ceremonial” is irrelevant to attention/concentration. • casually celebratory – positive tone; does not align with “erosion” (a negative decline).

questions.question: The word "manageable" most nearly means: (5)
options: Easy to handle or deal with
questions.explanation: The correct meaning is ‘Easy to handle or deal with’. • Manageable = capable of being controlled/handled; not too difficult (हिंदी: “संभालने योग्य/नियंत्रण में आने वाला”). • As per the context, the author says distraction need not be a fixed destiny; it can be addressed through training and habits—so it is a “manageable challenge.” Why the others are incorrect: • (a) Impossible to control — opposite meaning. • (c) Permanently damaging — “manageable” does not mean harmful; it means controllable. • (d) Entirely accidental — refers to chance, not the ability to handle something. Synonyms: controllable, feasible, tractable. Antonyms: unmanageable, uncontrollable, impossible.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice. The young artist confidently announced that she could complete the mural if she were given an extra day.
options: The young artist announced, “I can complete the mural if I am given an extra day.”
The young artist said, “She could complete the mural if she was given an extra day.”
The young artist announced, “I could complete the mural if I am given an extra day.”
The young artist announced, “I could complete the mural if I were given an extra day.”
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). To convert from indirect speech to direct speech, we restore the speaker’s exact words. In the reporting clause, “The young artist announced” indicates a statement. In direct speech, the pronoun she changes to I because the artist is speaking about herself. Also, the reported verb could becomes can when we write the original statement in direct speech (normal present ability/possibility). The conditional clause “if she were given an extra day” becomes “if I am given an extra day” in the direct form used here. Rule focus (Indirect to Direct): • Change pronouns according to the speaker. • Restore the original tense/modal as spoken in direct speech. • Use quotation marks and appropriate punctuation. Correct direct speech: The young artist announced, “I can complete the mural if I am given an extra day.” Why other options are incorrect: • B keeps “She” instead of “I” and uses “was given” (not matching the intended direct statement pattern). • C mixes “could” with “am given,” creating an inconsistent modal-time relationship for this intended direct form. • D uses “I could” and “I were given,” which may express a more hypothetical tone, but the given answer key expects the straightforward restoration with “can” and “am given.” So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Select the word opposite in meaning to the word given below. Antithesis
questions.explanation: • Given word – Antithesis: • Antithesis means the direct opposite; a contrast or opposition between two things/ideas. It is also used in rhetoric to show a sharp contrast. • (Hindi meaning: विपरीतता / प्रतिलोम / विरोधाभास) • Example: Poverty is the antithesis of wealth. • Correct antonym – Similarity: • Similarity means resemblance; the state of being alike or having common features. • (Hindi meaning: समानता / मिलते-जुलते गुण) • It is the opposite of “antithesis” because antithesis indicates opposition/contrast, while similarity indicates likeness. • Example: There is a striking similarity between the two designs. • Synonyms of Antithesis: opposite, contrast, contradiction, reverse. • Antonyms of Antithesis: similarity, likeness, agreement, conformity. • Meanings of other options (why they are incorrect): • (a) Antipole – a point directly opposite another; opposite pole (Hindi: विपरीत ध्रुव) → यह भी opposite ही दर्शाता है, इसलिए antonym नहीं है। • (b) Contrast – difference; opposition (Hindi: विरोध / अंतर) → यह antithesis का synonym है। • (d) Review – evaluation; re-examination (Hindi: समीक्षा) → अर्थ असंबंधित है। So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: The question below consists of a set of labelled (P, Q, R, S) sentences. Out of four options given, select the most logical order of the sentences which form a paragraph. By the early part of 1939 the German dictator Adolf Hitler had become determined to invade and occupy Poland. P. In a secret protocol of this pact, the Germans and the Soviets agreed that Poland should be divided between them, with the western third of the country going to Germany and the eastern two-thirds being taken over by the U.S.S.R. Q. Hitler intended to invade Poland anyway, but first he had to neutralize the possibility that the Soviet Union would resist the invasion of its western neighbour. R. Poland, for its part, had guarantees of French and British military support should it be attacked by Germany. S. Secret negotiations led on August 23–24 to the signing of the German-Soviet Nonaggression Pact in Moscow. Having achieved this cynical agreement, the other provisions of which stupefied Europe even without divulgence of the secret protocol, Hitler thought that Germany could attack Poland with no danger of Soviet or British intervention and gave orders for the invasion to start on August 26.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (d) RQSP. Explanation (logical flow): • The opening sentence states Hitler’s determination to invade Poland. The paragraph must then explain the situation and the steps taken before invasion. • Sentence (R) fits first because it gives Poland’s position and the international context: Poland had British and French guarantees if attacked. • Sentence (Q) must follow because it explains Hitler’s key concern despite these guarantees: he needed to neutralize possible Soviet resistance before invading. • Sentence (S) comes next as the action taken to address that concern: secret negotiations led to the German-Soviet Nonaggression Pact. • Sentence (P) naturally follows (S) because it refers to this pact and explains the secret protocol dividing Poland, which directly supports the concluding line about Hitler’s confidence. Why other options are incorrect: • (a) RSPQ: Places the pact (S) before explaining why Hitler needed it (Q), which weakens cause-and-effect. • (b) RQPS: Explains the secret protocol (P) before introducing the pact (S); the reference this pact becomes unclear. • (c) RPSQ: Places the protocol (P) too early and again uses this pact without proper introduction. So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. The landowners sold the abandoned land to the Jewish Land Fund at an outrageous price.
options: The abandoned land was sold by the landowners to the Jewish Land Fund at an outrageous price.
The abandoned land had been sold by the landowners to the Jewish Land Fund at an outrageous price.
The Jewish Land Fund was sold by the landowners to abandoned land at an outrageous price.
The abandoned land sold the landowners to the Jewish Land Fund at an outrageous price.
questions.explanation: Correct Passive Sentence: · The abandoned land was sold by the landowners to the Jewish Land Fund at an outrageous price. Explanation: · The given sentence is in simple past tense (sold). · While converting active to passive voice: · The obwect of the active sentence becomes the subwect of the passive sentence. · The tense remains the same. · The verb changes to was/were + past participle (V³). Grammatical Rule Used (English): · Active: Subwect + V² + obwect · Passive: Obwect + was/were + V³ + by + subwect Grammatical Rule Used (Hindi): · Simple past tense के passive voice में · verb की second form → was/were + verb की third form · Active का obwect, passive का subwect बनता है। Explanation of Other Options: · (b): Uses past perfect passive ( had been sold), which changes the tense. · (c): Meaning is illogical; subwect–obwect roles are wrong. · (d): Grammatically incorrect; active verb used with passive structure.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. When the celebrity was asked about his controversy, he ________ denied to save his status.
questions.explanation: Solution: The blank needs an adverb that intensifies the verb “denied” and matches the context of a celebrity trying to protect his image. Vehemently means strongly, forcefully, and with intense feeling. Saying someone “vehemently denied” something is a standard and grammatically correct collocation, especially when the person is under pressure to maintain reputation. (Hindi meaning: जोरदार ढंग से/तीव्रता से) Example: The politician vehemently denied the allegations during the press conference. Why other options are incorrect: • Option A: concurrently – Means simultaneously; it does not fit with “denied” in this context. (Hindi meaning: साथ-साथ/एक साथ) • Option B: accurately – Means correctly/precisely; denial is not described in terms of accuracy here. (Hindi meaning: सटीक रूप से) • Option D: happily – Means with happiness; it contradicts the serious tone of controversy and denial. (Hindi meaning: खुशी से) So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Some parts of a passage have been wumbled up. Select the option that gives the correct sequence. Royal Enfield was a brand name under which The Enfield Cycle Company Limited sold motorcycles. P. The first Royal Enfield motorcycle was built in 1901. Q. In 1955, Enfield Cycle Company partnered with Madras Motors in India. R. Enfield Cycle Company also used the brand name Enfield without Royal. S. The Enfield Cycle Company is responsible for the design and original production of the Royal Enfield Bullet.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (d) RPSQ. Explanation (sequence logic): • R should come first because it adds an immediate detail about branding: the company also used “Enfield” without “Royal”, which naturally follows the introductory line about the brand name. • P follows by giving the earliest historical milestone (built in 1901), which is logically the first event in the timeline. • S then adds a significant product-related fact about the company’s role in designing and producing the Bullet. • Q comes last as a later timeline event (1955 partnership in India), making it a suitable concluding historical point. Why other options are incorrect: • (a) SRQP places the product detail before the first motorcycle milestone, weakening the timeline flow. • (b) SPQR places the 1955 partnership too early and shifts from early history to later history abruptly. • (c) QSRP begins with a 1955 event, which cannot logically follow an introduction without first mentioning earlier history. “So the correct answer is (d)”

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of homonyms can be used to fill in the blanks most suitably? When the weweller polished his ____ ring, he noticed a young ____ gliding near the reeds by the lake.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). The sentence needs two words that sound similar but have different meanings and fit the context. A weweller would polish a signet ring, which is a ring bearing a seal or emblem, often used to stamp wax. Near a lake, a young swan is called a cygnet. These two words are commonly confused in sound but have entirely different meanings, so they form the most suitable pair here. signet (Hindi meaning: “मुहर वाली अंगूठी”) and cygnet (Hindi meaning: “हंस का बच्चा”). Example: He wore a signet ring with his family crest. Example: A cygnet swam beside its mother in the lake. Why other options are incorrect: • A symbol/cymbal do not fit: a ring is not “symbol ring” in this exact sense, and “cymbal” is a musical instrument, not a bird. • C reverses the required meanings (a weweller polishes a ring, not a cygnet). • D cymbal/symbol again do not match the lake context and ring context appropriately. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Four words are given, out of which only one word is spelt correctly. Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: • Explanation: The correctly spelt word is LOGORRHEA. • This word comes from Greek roots: • logo- (words/speech) + -rrhea (flow/discharge) • इसलिए इसकी सही spelling में double “r” (rr) आता है, और ending “-rhea” होती है। • Correct word – LOGORRHEA: • Meaning: Excessive and often incoherent talkativeness; a tendency to use too many words. • (Hindi meaning: अत्यधिक बोलना / शब्दों की बाढ़ / अनर्गल वाचालता) • Example: In his nervousness, he slipped into logorrhea and kept speaking without pause.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ‘one word’ for the expressions given below A devoted supporter of a group, cause, or person
questions.explanation: The correct one-word substitution is (c) Partisan. • Given expression – A devoted supporter of a group, cause, or person: This refers to a person who strongly supports one side, party, leader, or cause and shows loyalty towards it. • Correct answer word – Partisan: Partisan means a strong supporter of a party, cause, or person. • (Hindi meaning: पक्षपाती / समर्थक) • Example: He is a partisan of the reform movement and defends it strongly. Meanings of all the other given options: • Zeal – great enthusiasm (उत्साह) • Veracity – truthfulness (सत्यता) • Tacit – implied, not spoken (अप्रत्यक्ष / मौन) So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the highlighted part of the given sentence. India went forward all odds and won the match against West Indies.
questions.explanation: The correct substitution of the highlighted words is (a) against. Explanation: The phrase should be “India went against all odds,” which means to face and overcome difficulties. “Forward” is incorrect here both idiomatically and grammatically. Grammatical Rule Used: · The preposition “against” is used to indicate opposition or resistance. · “Against all odds” is a standard phrase meaning “despite the difficulties”. Example: He succeeded against all odds. Information Booster: “Go against” is a phrasal verb meaning “to oppose or resist”.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate one word for the expression given below. The branch of philosophy concerned with the theory of knowledge
questions.explanation: • Given expression: “The branch of philosophy concerned with the theory of knowledge” refers to the study of knowledge, including its nature, sources, limits, and validity—i.e., how we know what we know. • Correct one-word substitution – Epistemology: • Epistemology is the branch of philosophy that studies knowledge and belief, especially the nature and basis of knowledge. • (Hindi meaning: ज्ञानमीमांसा / ज्ञान का सिद्धांत) • Example: Epistemology examines whether knowledge comes from reason, experience, or both. • Meanings of other options (why they are incorrect): • (b) Atmology – study of vapour/steam (rare term) (Hindi: वाष्प-विज्ञान / भाप का अध्ययन) • (c) Seismology – study of earthquakes (Hindi: भूकंप विज्ञान) • (d) Polemology – study of war and conflict (Hindi: युद्ध-विज्ञान / युद्ध का अध्ययन) So the correct answer is (a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given phrase. A sledgehammer to crack a nut
options: To have a lot of options to fulfil a task
A person of very high stature
To use disproportionate force or expense to overcome a minor problem
A person performing a task against his demeanour
questions.explanation: • Given phrase – “A sledgehammer to crack a nut”: • This phrase means using a very strong, heavy, or expensive method to deal with a very small or simple problem. • In other words, the action taken is far greater than what the situation actually requires. • (Hindi meaning: छोटी समस्या के लिए जरूरत से ज्यादा बल/खर्च लगाना; तोप से मक्खी मारना) • Example: Hiring a full legal team for a minor complaint is like using a sledgehammer to crack a nut. • Correct meaning – Option (c): • “To use disproportionate force or expense to overcome a minor problem” exactly matches the sense of the phrase because a sledgehammer is far too powerful for cracking a small nut. • Other related idioms/phrases: • Make a mountain out of a molehill – exaggerate a small issue (Hindi: राई का पहाड़ बनाना) • Overkill – more action than necessary (Hindi: जरूरत से ज्यादा करना) • To kill a fly with a cannon – excessive method for a tiny task (Hindi: तोप से मक्खी मारना) • Meanings of other options (why they are incorrect): • (a) “a lot of options” – unrelated to using excessive force. • (b) “person of very high stature” – unrelated meaning. • (d) “performing a task against demeanour” – does not match the idea of excessive measures. So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect narration of the following statement from the given alternatives: Mohan said, “I will come to the library this evening if my friend does not take me to the market.”
options: Mohan said that he will come to the library that evening if his friend did not take him to the market.
Mohan said that he would come to the library that evening if his friend did not take him to the market.
Mohan said that he would go to the library this evening if his friend does not take him to the market.
Mohan said that he came to the library that evening if his friend had not taken him to the market.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). Explanation: The given sentence is in Direct Speech with the reporting verb “said” (past tense). While converting to Indirect Speech (Reported Speech), the following rules are applied: • Rule 1 (Backshifting of tense): When the reporting verb is in the past, will generally changes to would. • Rule 2 (Pronoun change): “I” changes to he because Mohan is the speaker. • Rule 3 (Time expression change): “this evening” changes to that evening. • Rule 4 (Conditional clause): In a real condition, the if-clause can remain in past form as shown: “does not take” becomes “did not take” due to sequence of tenses in reporting. Why option (b) is correct: Option (b) correctly changes will come to would come, changes the time phrase to that evening, and correctly changes pronouns: “me” to “him”. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (a) is incorrect because it keeps will come instead of changing it to would come. • Option (c) is incorrect because it uses go instead of come and it wrongly keeps “this evening” and “does not take” without proper reporting changes. • Option (d) is incorrect because it changes the meaning and tense improperly by using came and had not taken, which does not match the original conditional future sense. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: The diligent worker performs the assigned duties with great precision every single day.
options: The assigned duties are performed with great precision by the diligent worker every single day.
The assigned duties were performed with great precision by the diligent worker every single day.
The assigned duties are being performed with great precision by the diligent worker every single day.
The diligent worker is performed with great precision by the assigned duties every single day.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). The given sentence is in Simple Present Tense active voice: “The diligent worker performs the assigned duties...”. To form the passive voice in simple present, we use: Structure: Obwect + am/is/are + V3 (past participle) + by + Subwect Here, the obwect “the assigned duties” becomes the subwect in passive voice, and since “duties” is plural, we use are. The verb “perform” becomes “performed” (V3). Therefore, option (A) is correct. Correct passive sentence: The assigned duties are performed with great precision by the diligent worker every single day. Why other options are incorrect: • B uses were performed (simple past), which changes the tense. • C uses are being performed (present continuous passive), which changes the tense/aspect. • D incorrectly makes the doer “worker” the subwect of being performed, which is illogical and ungrammatical. So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate one-word substitution for the bracketed word from the below sentence. (We had picked the baby up) and went straight to the police station.
options: We have picked the baby up
questions.explanation: The correct substitution of the highlighted words is (b) We picked the baby up. Explanation: In a single narrative about consecutive past actions connected by and, the standard tense is Simple Past for both actions: picked … went. Using Past Perfect (had picked) is wustified only to show an action completed before another past reference point explicitly mentioned (e.g., By the time the police arrived, we had picked the baby up). Here, no earlier past reference point exists; both actions occur in sequence, so Simple Past is appropriate and stylistically concise. Options (c) and (d) use perfect aspects that tie the action to the present time frame, which conflicts with the past narrative anchored by went. Option (a) is a fragment. Grammatical rule used: · Sequence of past actions: When two past actions occur one after another and there is no explicit earlier past marker, use Simple Past for both. · Past Perfect (had + V3): Use to indicate an action completed before another specific past time/action. · Example: We locked the door and left (both Simple Past). / We had locked the door before the guests arrived (Past Perfect + Simple Past). · Information booster / exceptions: Past Perfect is common with time markers like by the time, before, after, or when clarifying chronology in complex narratives. Overuse without a clear earlier reference point is considered non-idiomatic.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate phrasal verb. Due to mounting pressure at work and multiple deadlines to meet, she felt completely overwhelmed and finally decided to ____ some of her responsibilities to her assistant.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The context shows she is overwhelmed and wants to transfer duties to her assistant. Therefore, the phrasal verb must mean “to transfer/entrust responsibility to someone else” (Hindi meaning: सौंप देना / जिम्मेदारी देना). Why option (C) is correct: Hand over means to give control, responsibility, or possession to another person (Hindi: हस्तांतरित करना). It perfectly fits “hand over responsibilities.” Example: The manager decided to hand over the file to the assistant. Why other options are incorrect: • Option A (take up): to begin an activity or accept a task (Hindi: शुरू करना / अपनाना)—opposite meaning here. • Option B (hand down): to pass something from older generation to younger or to announce an official decision (Hindi: पीढ़ी दर पीढ़ी देना)—not suitable. • Option D (give in): to surrender or yield (Hindi: हार मान लेना)—does not mean transfer responsibilities. “So the correct answer is (c)”
